notes: TARI v2 cross-transcript run. 5 frontier-research talks. Transcript T001 (Belinda Li) is reused from run_001. Other 4 added for v2.

transcripts.speaker: Belinda Li
transcripts.speaker_affiliation: MIT EECS (PhD student, advisor Jacob Andreas)
transcripts.topic: Building AI systems with coherent and updatable models of the world, user, and themselves
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Talk timing overlaps Claude knowledge cutoff (Jan 2026); content includes prior published work by speaker (e.g., arXiv:2406.19501 propositional probes, arXiv:2503.02854 state tracking) that is likely within Claude's training corpus.

transcripts.speaker: Yu Sun
transcripts.topic: Test-time training and adaptive computation at inference
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. Test-time training and related sequence modeling (TTT layers, RNNs as TTT) are widely published 2024-2025; speaker's own work likely indexed.

transcripts.speaker: Nicholas Roberts
transcripts.speaker_affiliation: University of Wisconsin (finishing PhD); CMU MS prior
transcripts.topic: Science of foundation models for science (FMs as scientific instruments)
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. FMs-for-science is an active research area 2024-2026 with substantial published work.

transcripts.speaker: Valerie Chen
transcripts.speaker_affiliation: CMU ML department (PhD student, advisor Amit Kawalka)
transcripts.topic: Human-AI collaboration: measuring and improving how AI works with people
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. NOTE: this transcript has unusual sentence structure with very few sentence-final punctuation marks (only 35 sentences in 47KB); snippet/atom yield will be lower than other transcripts.

transcripts.speaker: Amrith Setlur
transcripts.topic: Training models to adapt at test time via reinforcement learning
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. Test-time RL and RL-trained reasoning models (DeepSeek-R1 family) are 2024-2026 frontier topics with extensive published work.

expected_collisions: T002 (Yu Sun) and T005 (Amrith Setlur) both discuss test-time adaptation — pair likely to produce candidates with high overlap with TTT literature.
T001 (Belinda Li) and T002 (Yu Sun) both touch latent world models / state tracking — possible adjacency.
T003 (Nicholas Roberts) discusses foundation models for science — may collide with FM-as-science-instrument literature regardless of pairing.
expected_diverse_pairs: T001 x T003: world/user/self models with FMs-for-science is unusual.
T001 x T004: self-model interpretability with human-AI collaboration is unusual.
T003 x T004: FMs-for-science with human-AI collaboration may produce 'how do scientists collaborate with model' candidates.
